title: Knights
description:
Write a program to solve logic puzzles about knights and knaves. Knights always tell the truth, knaves always lie. Given a set of statements, determine who is a knight and who is a knave by encoding each puzzle as a propositional logic sentence and using a model-checking algorithm.

You must implement the `And`, `Or`, `Not`, `Implication`, and `Biconditional` classes in `logic.py`.
common_mistakes: Incorrectly encoding implication (A → B is not the same as A AND B)
Forgetting to include the constraint that each character is exactly one of knight/knave
Not handling the Biconditional as (A → B) AND (B → A)

title: Minesweeper
description:
Write an AI to play Minesweeper using propositional logic. The AI maintains a knowledge base of logical sentences and uses inference to mark safe cells and mines.

You must implement `add_knowledge`, `make_safe_move`, and `make_random_move` in `MinesweeperAI`.
common_mistakes: Not updating the knowledge base when a new mine or safe cell is discovered
Failing to infer new sentences by subtracting known subsets
Adding duplicate sentences to the knowledge base causing redundant computation
